title: Test Agent einrichten

commits.message: [agent:engineer][task:ops-0014] fix CI submodule checkout for test and review workflows
commits.author: engineer

handoff_notes: Review found multiple clean-clone CI defects. Commit e610a39 fixed recursive submodule checkout and the missing metrics-collector .gitmodules entry. Commit 58f1fae switched submodule URLs to HTTPS and corrected the Python package install path. Commit 231b96c narrowed submodule fetches per job and fixed broken review-agent YAML. Commit 886df71 fixed the remaining Tests failures. The final red path was Review Agent workflow configuration: its PR comment steps were still failing under GitHub integration restrictions, and CodeQL upload was invalid because code scanning is not enabled for this repository. The current follow-up branch makes comment publication best-effort and aligns Review Agent with the repository's real GitHub capabilities. Awaiting GitHub Actions validation before closing the task.
review_notes: Review on the merged clean checkout found real CI defects. First pass: submodule-backed paths were empty in a clean clone, and metrics-collector was missing from .gitmodules. Second pass: .gitmodules still used SSH-only GitHub URLs, which commonly break Actions submodule checkout, and the Python workflow installed from repo root even though the package root is projects/digital-research/src. Third pass: even with those fixes, recursive checkout remained wrong because it forces GitHub Actions to fetch private cross-repo working-notes. Fourth pass: after targeted checkout, the remaining failures were project-local dependency issues: missing numpy during digital-research test collection and no package-lock.json for metrics-collector. Fifth pass: Tests turned green, but Review Agent still failed because the workflow assumed unavailable repository features. The follow-up branch now disables CodeQL upload until code scanning is enabled and treats PR comments as best-effort instead of a blocking requirement.
